source_metadata.title: Public Advanced NSCLC Trial Eligibility Criteria

content_blocks.text: This JSON supports preliminary screening only. Final enrollment decisions belong to the study site and trial team.

items: Cancer type: non-small cell lung cancer
Allowed stage group: stage IIIB or IIIC locally advanced unresectable disease, or stage IV metastatic disease
Known actionable genomic alterations are allowed
Examples include EGFR mutation, ALK rearrangement, and other actionable alterations
If an actionable alteration is present, at least one relevant targeted therapy should already have been used when locally available
Required response state: progressed during, relapsed after, or was intolerant to the most recent therapy
If no known actionable alteration: prior platinum-based combination therapy for metastatic or recurrent disease and PD-(L)1 therapy unless contraindicated
Alternative path: progression within 6 months of last platinum dose in adjuvant, neoadjuvant, or chemoradiotherapy setting plus PD-(L)1 therapy
If a known actionable alteration is present: at least one relevant targeted therapy and platinum-based therapy or early progression after platinum
Another malignancy within 3 years except low-risk exceptions
Active CNS lesions unless definitively treated and stable
